evaluation_scope_note: To keep this illustration readable, candidate evaluation below is scoped to the six topics tracked in memory/examples/sample-memory-state.json plus each tracked topic's direct next_topics from the curriculum (the natural frontier around them), rather than all 81 roadmap topics. A real selector run evaluates the entire roadmap the same way; the algorithm itself is unchanged, only the example's candidate set is narrowed for clarity. educational_sequence_fit below is computed relative to position within this scoped set, not the full roadmap.

next_topic.reason: java-operators is the closest not-yet-covered topic to the curriculum frontier — it immediately follows the posted java-variables-and-data-types and its only prerequisite is already posted. It has never been used, and it inherits above-average momentum (performance_boost) from java-variables-and-data-types' strong 0.78 performance score. No audience demand data exists yet in Phase 1, so that factor was neutral across every candidate.

revision_topic.reason: The only topic currently at status needs_revisit, java-arrays-basics, is still inside its cooldown window (avoid_until: 2026-08-01, which is after this selection_timestamp). No other topic is flagged needs_revisit. A null selection here is expected behavior, not an error — there is no eligible revision candidate right now.

high_performance_topic.reason: java-method-basics posted with a 0.88 performance_score, above the 0.8 high-performance threshold, making its related_topics eligible for this mode. Both java-parameters-and-return and java-variable-scope inherit that momentum equally, but java-parameters-and-return wins narrowly on educational_sequence_fit — it is the very next topic listed after java-method-basics in the curriculum's methods-and-scope level.
high_performance_topic.note: java-parameters-and-return and java-variable-scope scored 0.438 and 0.436 respectively before rounding to two decimal places; the win is real but narrow, decided by educational_sequence_fit.

gap_filler_topic.reason: No audience demand signal exists yet in Phase 1 (see topic-ranking.json), so this mode currently falls back to ranking on curriculum proximity, novelty, and momentum alone — effectively behaving like next_topic restricted to unqueued topics, as documented. java-operators wins on the same basis as the next_topic decision. This result should be treated as weak until real audience feedback data exists to differentiate candidates.
